QuickWits

Remember those 'unfiltered thoughts' and 'hot takes' I mentioned? This is where they often land first, mostly streamed from my twitter feed. Raw, rapid-fire, and probably less polished than my SQL queries.

Last updated: July 13, 2026 · @batra99

July 2026

Parth Batra @batra99
Jul 11
ASML not being able to scale up with these unprecedented demands of lithography, has to be a generational fumble!

now china has perfected some of these supply chains issues and eventually they will figure it out, like always.

Parth Batra @batra99
Jul 01
maybe one of these deployments can fix lag in what song is currently playing when i connect it to different device over wifi like my home theatre.

youtube music works great tho. even though they might be using inferior gemini for coding.
Quoting @ClaudeDevs
Boris sat down with Spotify VP of Engineering Niklas Gustavsson. Spotify ships 4,500 production deploys a day…

Parth Batra @batra99
Jun 30
a trillion param model by a food delivery company in china.

these everyday companies in china have more compute resources and AI talent than most big nations in europe.

Introducing LongCat-2.0 — 1.6T parameters · MoE with ~48B active · 1M context. The full model behind Owl Alpha on @OpenRouter, now available. Built for agentic coding from the ground up…

Maybe the void isn't meant to be filled. Maybe it's just depth. Maybe it's space meant for oceans, not puddles. Maybe nothing was ever missing. Maybe I was just too vast to be contained by small comforts.

Diwali homecomings = “ghar ki raunak” = moms. Take them out and it’s just dusty homes, same old t-shirts, dal chawal and people pretending fairy lights count as efforts. In India, Moms are the festival

Parth Batra @batra99
Oct 12
Top 3 types of "songs": 1. The ones you never ever want to finish. 2. The one you're still humming all day. 3. The ones where you just remember the tune and it makes you smile. (No, I am not talking about songs here)

Parth Batra @batra99
Sep 25
“You only lose what you cling to.” – Buddha → Extending on this based on my recent readings ... imo life is just a brutal 'pick 2 out of 3' situation, and the options are always 1. Freedom 2. Attachment 3. Acceptance. ...and the correct answer changes multiple times a day based on who/what you're dealing with.
🔗 View Tweet
Parth Batra @batra99
Sep 15
Hot take: Ferrari is basically a merch company that also makes super cool cars & hires F1 legends just for the aesthetic/promo. Their car sales scale can't possibly be as profitable as those sweet merch collabs. Brand Empire, Not a car Empire.
🔗 View Tweet
Parth Batra @batra99
Sep 09
i mean... shouldn't the most unpatriotic thing actually be "blindly trusting the government" - without questioning anything they do?

Parth Batra @batra99
Sep 05
files over app. Always. Your notes app should let you bulk export, and bulk import from standard formats like markdown, txt etc. Used logseq for good 3 years but app had became buggy. Till i found a better alternative, Obsidian all the way.
🔗 View Tweet

August 2025

Parth Batra @batra99
Aug 29
that 'delta(p)' is what we should focus on. In my humble opinion, there is just one way: Minimize Unforced Error. This is also the trait in most pioneers in their craft around us - world champions, 10x engineers, best leaders. #fuckdeltap
🔗 View Tweet
Parth Batra @batra99
Aug 28
Net productivity, p=p0 - delta(p) - p0 is the ultimate productivity - delta(p) is the cumulation of various human factors which hampers the net result all these AI-tools, productivity tools focus on that p0 part, but that delta(p) is where magic happens!
🔗 View Tweet
Parth Batra @batra99
Aug 22
More important than being happy is being easygoing, i.e. being okay with whatever happens. This doesn’t preclude trying to change bad circumstances, but it does free you from that extra suffering that comes from knowing you’re suffering.
🔗 View Tweet
Parth Batra @batra99
Aug 15
Real Change vs Illusion of Change Most changes 'clearly' visible in short term are just 'Illusions of change". Real changes take so much time that even the people driving it dont notice in day-to-day until it compounds over long time - sometimes even months, years.
🔗 View Tweet

Perplexity has done such a good job with UI and User interactions that, every other app i see, even from Big4 are just using same interactions - that's what you call a good design.
Let alone interactions, even MetaAIs home page is straight up copied from pplx.

December 2024

Parth Batra @batra99
Dec 11
saw an inspiring video that said you need to have 4 hobbies.
create, consume, cavort, commune
create: bring something to life
consume: appreciate the art of another
cavort: move your body daily
commune: have a community to socialize with
